title: Oeonia macrostachya
taxonomy: Oeonia macrostachya is classified as a species within the genus Oeonia, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places Oeonia macrostachya in the order Asparagales. The family Orchidaceae is further divided into 763 genera, with Oeonia being one of them. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Oeonia macrostachya shares characteristics with other orchids, such as a unique flower structure. Oeonia macrostachya's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other orchids.

intro: Oenothera macrostachya, also known as evening primrose, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America. The plant is rich in health benefits, particularly in relieving symptoms of eczema, acne, and other skin conditions due to its high content of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a polyunsaturated fatty acid. The bioactive constituents of Oenothera macrostachya include GLA, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with evening primrose include oil extracts, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
